_note: Metadata-only slice of the OpenTabs yelp plugin, vendored hermetically at SHA 4b17021637d2cac12b8d84d21c40e765aa7b85e9 (see vendor/opentabs-snapshot/PIN.md). Wall 1: NO dist/, NO handle() runtime is executed -- the importer (scripts/import-opentabs-catalog.mjs) reads ONLY .name/.description/.input/.group/.summary metadata. The opentabs.urlPatterns below is the AUTHORITATIVE upstream value (*://www.yelp.com/* -> service www.yelp.com). www.yelp.com derives the host stem 'www', so the dir-name STEM_OVERRIDES {yelp:'yelp'} canonicalizes the slug to opentabs__yelp__* (the same first-label-isn't-the-app-name canonicalization as cloudflare/datadog/threads/doordash). www.yelp.com is left UNCLASSIFIED (SAFE) in extension/config/service-denylist.json -- it is a read-only local-reviews app (business search + reviews are reads; reads run under Auto with no mutating re-gate). It is added to verify-catalog-crosscheck.mjs READ_ONLY_SAFE_SERVICES (the 38 MED-02 guard) SPECIFICALLY because its vendored ops are read-only, so a FUTURE re-vendor adding a write op for yelp would FAIL the build (forcing an explicit re-classification). defineTool/OpenTabsPlugin and the api transport helper are resolved from local stubs (sdk-stub.ts, yelp-api.ts) so the importer's import() never drags the real SDK's DOM/fetch surface into node. backing:'dom' (the frozen conservative default) -> DOM-only routing. ALL ops are read (api {method:'GET'}); NO write op, NO payment verb -> the READ_ONLY_SAFE guard PASSES and the payment-op guard never keys on yelp.
name: @opentabs-dev/opentabs-plugin-yelp
